definitions.definition: Measures of central tendency are statistical tools used to describe the middle or average value of a dataset, providing an indication of the central point around which the data is distributed.
The sum of all observations divided by the number of observations, representing the average value of a dataset.
definitions.example: The mean height of a group of students is calculated by adding the heights of all students and dividing by the number of students.

definitions.definition: The middle value of a dataset when it is arranged in ascending or descending order, separating the higher half from the lower half.
definitions.example: In a dataset of exam scores, the median score is the middle value when all scores are arranged in order.

definitions.definition: The value that appears most frequently in a dataset, indicating the most common or typical value.
definitions.example: The mode of a dataset of exam scores is the score that appears most frequently in the dataset.

definitions.definition: The difference between the highest and lowest values in a dataset, indicating the spread or dispersion of the data.
definitions.example: The range of exam scores is the difference between the highest and lowest scores in the dataset.

definitions.definition: A value that divides the dataset into four equal parts, with 25% of the data below the first quartile (Q1), 50% below the second quartile (Q2, or median), and 75% below the third quartile (Q3).
definitions.example: The first quartile (Q1) of a dataset of exam scores is the value below which 25% of the scores fall.

definitions.definition: The difference between the third quartile (Q3) and the first quartile (Q1), indicating the spread or dispersion of the data.
definitions.example: The interquartile range (IQR) of a dataset of exam scores is the difference between the third quartile (Q3) and the first quartile (Q1).

definitions.definition: A dataset with values that are not evenly distributed around the mean, often resulting in a longer tail on one side of the distribution.
definitions.example: A dataset of exam scores with a skewed distribution may have a longer tail of lower scores.

definitions.definition: A dataset with values that are evenly distributed around the mean, resulting in a symmetrical shape.
definitions.example: A dataset of exam scores with a symmetric distribution may have a bell-shaped curve.

definitions.definition: A value that is significantly different from the other values in the dataset, often indicating an error or anomaly.
definitions.example: A student's exam score that is significantly higher or lower than the rest of the class may be considered an outlier.

definitions.definition: A measure of the amount of variation or dispersion in a dataset, indicating how spread out the data is from the mean.
definitions.example: The standard deviation of a dataset of exam scores indicates how spread out the scores are from the mean.

definitions.definition: A measure of the amount of variation or dispersion in a dataset, calculated as the average of the squared differences from the mean.
definitions.example: The variance of a dataset of exam scores is calculated by finding the average of the squared differences from the mean.

definitions.definition: The average of the absolute differences between each value and the mean, indicating the average distance from the mean.
definitions.example: The mean deviation of a dataset of exam scores is the average of the absolute differences between each score and the mean.

definitions.definition: The absolute difference between each value and the mean, indicating the distance from the mean.
definitions.example: The absolute deviation of a dataset of exam scores is the absolute difference between each score and the mean.

definitions.definition: A value that divides the dataset into 100 equal parts, with a certain percentage of the data below it.
definitions.example: The 25th percentile of a dataset of exam scores is the value below which 25% of the scores fall.

definitions.definition: A rule of thumb that suggests that about 68% of the data falls within 1 standard deviation of the mean, about 95% falls within 2 standard deviations, and about 99.7% falls within 3 standard deviations.
definitions.example: According to the range rule, about 95% of the exam scores in a dataset fall within 2 standard deviations of the mean.

definitions.definition: A graphical representation of a dataset, showing the median, quartiles, and outliers, used to visualize the shape and spread of the data.
definitions.example: A box plot of a dataset of exam scores shows the median, quartiles, and outliers, providing a visual representation of the data.

definitions.definition: A graphical representation of a dataset, showing the stem (the first part of the data value) and the leaf (the last part of the data value), used to visualize the shape and spread of the data.
definitions.example: A stem and leaf plot of a dataset of exam scores shows the stem and leaf of each score, providing a visual representation of the data.
